jsonLdWeb.name: Rebar Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate rebar bars, linear feet, weight, and chairs for concrete slab reinforcement.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate rebar for a concrete slab?

acceptedAnswer.text: For each direction, divide the slab dimension by the spacing and add 1. For a 20 ft × 30 ft slab with 12-inch spacing: lengthwise bars = 20/1 + 1 = 21 bars at 30 ft each, widthwise bars = 30/1 + 1 = 31 bars at 20 ft each. Total = 52 bars, 1,250 linear feet.

mainEntity.name: What rebar spacing should I use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common spacings are 12 inches (standard for driveways and structural slabs), 18 inches (patios and light-duty slabs), and 24 inches (non-structural slabs). Always follow your engineer's or local code specifications.

mainEntity.name: How much does rebar weigh per foot?

acceptedAnswer.text: #3 rebar (3/8") weighs 0.376 lb/ft, #4 rebar (1/2") weighs 0.668 lb/ft, and #5 rebar (5/8") weighs 1.043 lb/ft. Standard rebar comes in 20 ft lengths.

mainEntity.name: How many rebar chairs do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Place rebar chairs approximately every 3-4 feet along each bar to maintain proper elevation. For a typical slab, plan about 1 chair per 4 square feet. A 20×30 slab needs roughly 150 chairs.

mainEntity.name: What size rebar for a 4-inch slab?

acceptedAnswer.text: #3 (3/8") or #4 (1/2") rebar is standard for 4-inch residential slabs. #3 is common for patios and sidewalks. #4 is used for driveways and garage floors. Position rebar at the center of the slab thickness (2 inches from bottom).

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate overlap for rebar?

acceptedAnswer.text: When bars need to be spliced, overlap a minimum of 40 bar diameters. For #4 rebar: 40 × 0.5" = 20 inches overlap. For #5: 40 × 0.625" = 25 inches. Stagger splices so they don't all fall on the same line. This calculator adds 10% to linear feet to account for overlaps.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Rebar for a Slab

Rebar is laid in a grid pattern. Count bars in each direction based on the slab dimension and spacing, then calculate total linear feet and weight.

Bars per direction = Dimension ÷ Spacing + 1
  • Lengthwise bars: width ÷ spacing + 1, each bar = slab length
  • Widthwise bars: length ÷ spacing + 1, each bar = slab width
  • Linear feet: sum of all bar lengths + 10% for overlaps
  • 20 ft sticks: total linear feet ÷ 20
  • Weight: linear feet × weight per foot (varies by bar size)
  • Chairs: approximately 1 per 4 sq ft of slab area
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How do I calculate rebar for a slab?

Divide each slab dimension by the spacing and add 1. For 20×30 ft at 12" spacing: 21 bars lengthwise (30 ft each) + 31 bars widthwise (20 ft each) = 52 bars, 1,250 linear feet.

What rebar spacing should I use?

12" for driveways and structural slabs, 18" for patios, 24" for non-structural slabs. Always follow your engineer's specifications.

How much does rebar weigh per foot?

#3 = 0.376 lb/ft, #4 = 0.668 lb/ft, #5 = 1.043 lb/ft. Standard sticks are 20 ft long.

How many rebar chairs do I need?

About 1 chair per 4 square feet, placed every 3-4 ft along each bar. A 20×30 slab needs roughly 150 chairs.

What size rebar for a 4-inch slab?

#3 for patios and sidewalks, #4 for driveways and garages. Position at slab center (2" from bottom).

How much overlap for rebar splices?

Minimum 40 bar diameters: 15" for #3, 20" for #4, 25" for #5. Stagger splices across different lines.
